channel.title: morningNewsSection1
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from morningNewsSection1, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: How Carriers Use AI, Drones, and 'Cows' to Prepare for Hurricane Season
item.description: Though this hurricane season is expected to be milder, cellphone companies are still deploying all available resources.

item.title: A First Look Inside the Obama Presidential Center
item.description: The Obama Presidential Center opens later this month in Chicago. Here's a preview.

item.title: The $13 billion carrier with a plumbing problem is home. Now come the costly repairs
item.description: With 4,600 sailors finally home, USS Gerald R. Ford will finally receive some much needed repairs and an upgrade to its beleaguered sewage system.

item.title: More than 1 in 3 World Cup matches face dangerous heat risk, NPR analysis finds
item.description: Dangerously hot, humid weather is likely at many of the 2026 World Cup soccer venues. We crunched the numbers to see which matches are most at risk.
